title: Earnings call transcript: Hut 8 Q1 2026 reports major revenue growth
text: Hut 8 Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary. Revenue: $71 million, 226% year-over-year increase. Gross margins expanded to 64%, up from 14% prior year. Net loss: $253.1 million, primarily from non-cash mark-to-market adjustments on digital assets. EPS: -$0.362, beating forecasts of -$0.4088. Revenue missed guidance at $96.71 million versus forecast of $106.82 million. Beacon Point AI Data Center Campus: 15-year triple-net lease, 352 MW IT capacity (500 MW utility), investment-grade counterparty, $9.8 billion base-term contract value with 3% annual escalators. Riverbend Financing: Closed $3.25 billion investment-grade senior secured notes, 16.5-year fully amortizing at 6.192% coupon, BBB- ratings from S&P and Fitch. Recycled $184 million equity. Construction Timeline: Q2 2027 targeted delivery for Riverbend initial data hall. 8.4 GW development pipeline. Forward guidance: Continued losses projected for Q2 2026 (-$0.54 EPS) and Q3 2026 (-$0.63 EPS). Digital asset volatility and regulatory changes remain headwinds. Stock surged 31.87% after announcement, closing at $144.36.
